titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the John Levin collection of political activism for the Progressive Labor Party
Levin (John) Collection of political activism for the Progressive Labor Party
titlestmt.author: Elizabeth Nosari

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Describing Archives: A Content Standard

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday March 20, 2025 at 23:01

did.unittitle: John Levin collection of political activism for the Progressive Labor Party

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Chiefly in English. Some print material in Spanish and Russian.

did.abstract: This collection documents John Levin's activities with the Progressive Labor Party (PLP) during the 1960s and 1970s, primarily in California's San Francisco Bay Area. It contains Levin's papers in the form of correspondence, drafts, publications, and subject files in addition to records and publications of the PLP, Students for a Democratic Society (SDS), and additional mass organizations.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Collection materials are open for research.
Box 22 (audiovisual material/CD-R storage): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Andover Street Archives on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2023.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized in four series: I. John Levin papers, 1965-2019. II. Progressive Labor Party (PLP) records, 1962-1977. III. Students for a Democratic Society (SDS) records, 1965-1974. IV. Records of additional political organizations and front groups, 1964-1977.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Freeman Levin was born in New York, New York, on January 1, 1944, to writer Jane Whitebread Levin and lawyer Lester M. Levin. In 1966, he graduated from Columbia University, joined the Marxist-Leninist Progressive Labor Party (PLP), and relocated to San Francisco, California. Also in 1966, Levin enrolled in an MFA program in writing at San Francisco State College and was appointed by the party as chair of the PL Club. At SF State, Levin helped restore the school's Students for a Democratic Society (SDS) chapter where he was active in the PLP's section of SDS known as the Worker Student Alliance (WSA).

During his time as a PLP activist, Levin led and contributed to flier and leaflet campaigns, strikes, teach-ins, forums, demonstrations, and recruitment efforts to promote the party, activities that included working with the SDS and other mass organizations. He was a key figure in a number of actions, 1966-circa 1973, primarily related to the anti-war student movement and the labor movement, including leading the Bay Area's Stop the Draft Week in 1967 and generating student support for the United Farm Workers (UFW) grape boycott, 1965-1970. Levin was also one of the leaders of the SF State Strike, 1968-1969, in support of the anti-racist demands of the Third World Liberation Front and Black Student Union. He was arrested in 1968 for his strike activities and served a prison sentence. As the PLP's West Coast student organizer, Levin's reach extended beyond the Bay Area to help establish and lead other SDS/PLP campus chapters in Utah and Hawaii, among other locations. Levin also wrote PLP materials and contributed to party publications, including PL: Progressive Labor ("Power in the University," 1967, Vol. 6, No. 2) and Challenge ("S.F. State Rebellion!," coauthored with Hari Dillon, circa 1968).

In his autobiographical account, "'On Strike! Shut It Down!,'" Levin notes his disillusionment with internal party politics led to his dwindling participation in the PLP after the conclusion of the SF State Strike in 1969. He ultimately left the party in the 1970s and went on to establish a career as a playwright, novelist, and journalist. His published works include the novel The Great Divide , 1982 (coauthored with Frank M. Robinson); Three Plays: Veracruz, Snowbirds, Big Money , 2015; and "'On Strike! Shut It Down!'" in You Say You Want a Revolution: SDS, PL, and Adventures in Building a Worker-Student Alliance , 2019 (coedited with Earl Silbar).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Levin Collection of Political Activism for the Progressive Labor Party. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: In the subject files subseries of Series I, folder titles transcribed directly from Levin's original folders are enclosed in quotation marks. This also applies to the folder, "National Convention - Students for a Democratic Society (SDS)," 1967, undated, in Series III. All other folders titles are devised by the archivist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection documents John Levin's political interests and activities with the Marxist-Leninist Progressive Labor Party (PLP)—primarily in California's San Francisco Bay Area from the mid-1960s to the mid-1970s. It also includes research related to Levin's later publication You Say You Want a Revolution: SDS, PL, and Adventures in Building a Worker-Student Alliance , 2019 (coedited with Earl Silbar). Levin's papers consist of his correspondence, notebooks, writing drafts, print publications, and collected third-party publications. The majority is composed of subject files relating to his research interests and political activities in the anti-Vietnam War student movement, labor movement, and Black liberation movement, including his participation in the SF State Strike, 1968-1969. Subject files include Levin's notes, news clippings, internal party records, and political print ephemera (e.g., fliers, leaflets, and pamphlets) primarily from the PLP as well as Students for a Democratic Society (SDS) and other groups; they also include some photographs and SF State Strike-related digital A/V files. The remainder of the collection consists of internal party records, printed ephemera, and publications of the PLP, SDS, and additional political organizations and front groups.

This collection contains publications and printed ephemera with images of police brutality and violence, particularly in the context of Black liberation and the civil rights movement, as well as some instances of harmful and racist language.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The John Levin collection of political activism for the Progressive Labor Party is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized in four subseries: Correspondence, 1966-1970; Writing, notebooks, and oral history, 1966-2019; Subject files, 1965-2019; and Collected publications, 1967-1977. Subseries are arranged chronologically except for subject files, which are arranged alphabetically by folder title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I. John Levin papers, 1965-2019, consists of his correspondence, 1966-1970; notebooks, 1966-circa 1971; writing drafts, 1966-1969; a transcript of an oral history interview, 1984; publications, 1967-2019; and collected third-party publications, 1967-1977. The majority is comprised of subject files, 1965-2019, related to Levin's political activities (e.g., "SF State Strike, "Stop the Draft Week, "Lettuce Boycott," and a FOIA-requested copy of Levin's FBI surveillance file) and interests (e.g., "Black Liberation" and "Conscientious Objectors"). These files contain notes and news clippings as well as some correspondence, photographs, SF State Strike-related A/V files, and an SF State Strike reunion button. Subject files also include printed ephemera and internal party documents of the Progressive Labor Party (PLP), Students for a Democratic Society (SDS), and other organizations.

did.unittitle: Levin, John, "Power in the University," PL: Progressive Labor (vol. 6, no. 2).
Levin, John, and Hari Dillon. "S.F. State Rebellion!," Challenge .
Robinson, Frank M., and John Levin. The Great Divide . New York: Rawson, Wade.
Oral history interview transcript with annotations - Columbia University
Robinson, Frank M., and John Levin. The Great Divide [Russian language version]. Moscow: International Relations.
Levin, John F. Three Plays by John F. Levin: Veracruz, Snowbirds, Big Money . Black Apollo Press.
Levin, John F., and Earl Silbard, eds. You Say You Want a Revolution . San Francisco: 1741 Press.
"Articles on Students for a Democratic Society (SDS)"
"Eulogy for Alice Jerome by Son Fred Jerome"
"FBI Case File" for John F. Levin - FOIA request and photocopy
"National Committee - Criticism of Cuba for Siding with Soviets"
"PLP is Economist - P. Milkman / Reply R. Taus"
SF State Strike - DVD-R with news footage

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: The Turning Point SF State '68 (News footage from KQED+KPIX) 5-6 min Labor Video Project
SF State Strike - 20-year reunion materials, including button
SF State Strike - 20-year reunion materials, including button
You Say You Want a Revolution - press release and reviews

Committee on Internal Security, House of Representatives, Ninety-First Congress, Second Session. Anatomy of a Revolutionary Movement: "Students for a Democratic Society." Washington: U.S. Government Printing Office
Rubin, Jerry. Do It! New York: Simon and Schuster
Rubin, Jerry. We Are Everywhere . New York: Harper and Row
Adelson, Alan. SDS: A Profile . New York: Charles Scribner's Sons
Oglesby, Carl. The Yankee and Cowboy War . Berkeley Medallion
Series II : Progressive Labor Party (PLP) records

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: First, arranged alphabetically by record type. This is followed by Progressive Labor Party publications, also arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Revolution Today: USA . New York: Exposition Press
Series III: Students for a Democratic Society (SDS) records

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: First, arranged alphabetically by record type. This is followed by the Students for a Democratic Society publication SDS: New Left Notes .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III. Students for a Democratic Society records, 1965-1974, consists of conference and national convention materials, newsletters, bulletins, reports, statements, papers, resolutions, and proposals. It also includes pamphlets, fliers, and leaflets as well as issues of the SDS publication, SDS: New Left Notes , 1968-1972.

did.unittitle: "National Convention - Students for a Democratic Society (SDS)"
Series IV: Records of additional political organizations and front groups

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by name of the publishing organization.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series IV. Records of additional political organizations and front groups, 1964-1977, consists of a number of mass organizations and committees, including the Peace and Freedom Party, Communist Party, U.S.A, W. E. B. Du Bois Club, and Iranian Students' Association in the United States (ISAUS), among others. This series also contains materials from PLP front group the May 2nd Movement (M2M) as well as local Bay-Area committees and initiatives (e.g., the Berkeley Anti-Draft Union and Mission Tenants' Union). It is primarily composed of printed ephemera (e.g., fliers, leaflets, and pamphlets) in addition to some publications, including the Canadian Labour Party's The Worker , 1977, and the Revolutionary Union's The Red Papers , 1965-1972.

did.unittitle: Ad-Hoc Committee Against Nazism and Racism
Ad Hoc Group for American Anarchism
Canadian Party of Labour - The Worker
City College of San Francisco Student Coalition
Committee to Defend Resistance to Ghetto Life (CERGE)
